REQ-0002.original_text: The VDS supports real-time datamonitoring activities.
REQ-0002.final_text: The VDS supports real-time datamonitoring activities.

classification.justification: This requirement describes a core capability or action the system is designed to perform.
This requirement describes a core capability or action the system is designed to perform.

REQ-0004.original_text: The data distributed via the software system enables research in manufacturing-related domains.
REQ-0004.final_text: The data distributed via the software system enables research in manufacturing-related domains.

classification.justification: This requirement describes the intended capability and purpose of the data provided by the system.

REQ-0005.original_text: The distributed data also supports the optimization and enhancement of practitioner processes.
REQ-0005.final_text: The distributed data also supports the optimization and enhancement of practitioner processes.

classification.justification: This requirement describes a capability of the distributed data system to support process optimization.

REQ-0006.original_text: Lastly, the distributed data supports solution-provider development activities, such as designing, coding, testing, and debugging software applications.
REQ-0006.final_text: Lastly, the distributed data supports solution-provider development activities, such as designing, coding, testing, and debugging software applications.

classification.justification: Describes the operational purpose of the data support, enabling development activities.

REQ-0007.original_text: The VDS must provide a report of all data items available in the VDS
REQ-0007.final_text: The VDS must provide a report of all data items available in the VDS

classification.justification: This requirement describes a capability the system must perform: generating a report of available data items.

REQ-0008.original_text: The VDS must provide a near-real-time stream of most current value for each data item
REQ-0008.final_text: The VDS must provide a near-real-time stream of most current value for each data item

classification.justification: This requirement describes a capability (providing a stream) whose point is the timing bound of 'near-real-time'.

REQ-0009.original_text: The VDS must provide a time series of most recent values collected for each data item available in the VDS buffer
REQ-0009.final_text: The VDS must provide a time series of most recent values collected for each data item available in the VDS buffer

classification.justification: Describes a capability of the VDS to provide specific data.

REQ-0011.original_text: Provide a data stream through the VDS in a form compliant with MTConnect and data templates in the QDR
REQ-0011.final_text: Provide a data stream through the VDS in a form compliant with MTConnect and data templates in the QDR

classification.justification: Defines the required format and protocol for data exchange with an external system.

REQ-0012.original_text: Upload, edit, and create data templates
REQ-0012.final_text: Upload, edit, and create data templates

classification.justification: Describes a set of capabilities related to data template management.

REQ-0013.original_text: Make all data available over a RESTful API
REQ-0013.final_text: Make all data available over a RESTful API

classification.justification: Defines a capability (making data available) that must be exposed via a specific external interface (RESTful API).

REQ-0014.original_text: Provide a User Interface (UI) for administration and managing templates and queries
REQ-0014.final_text: Provide a User Interface (UI) for administration and managing templates and queries

classification.justification: Describes a capability (providing a UI) whose primary intent is user interaction.

REQ-0015.original_text: Allow the administrator to create predefined user queries with limits set on results by a time range (e.g., number of days, hours, minutes), a limited set of query-able data elements, or a limited set of records
REQ-0015.final_text: Allow the administrator to create predefined user queries with limits set on results by a time range (e.g., number of days, hours, minutes), a limited set of query-able data elements, or a limited set of records

classification.justification: This describes a capability (creating queries) that is bounded by limits on result size, data scope, and time range.
This is a request for metadata or a capability to list elements, which is classified as a general function.

REQ-0017.original_text: The server-side components of the software system must operate within a Linux operating system environment
REQ-0017.final_text: The server-side components of the software system must operate within a Linux operating system environment

classification.justification: Specifies the required execution environment for the system's components.

REQ-0018.original_text: The client-side components of the software system must operate within common webbrowser environments using Secure Sockets Layer (SSL) / Transport Layer Security (TLS) cryptographic protocols at a minimum encryption level of 128 bits
REQ-0018.final_text: The client-side components of the software system must operate within common webbrowser environments using Secure Sockets Layer (SSL) / Transport Layer Security (TLS) cryptographic protocols at a minimum encryption level of 128 bits

classification.justification: This requirement mandates an external communication protocol (SSL/TLS) and sets a specific cryptographic bound (128 bits) for interoperability.
This requirement specifies a compatibility constraint regarding supported software versions.
Specifies a minimum required operating environment or compatibility level.

REQ-0021.original_text: All QDR and administrative services must be available over a RESTful API
REQ-0021.final_text: All QDR and administrative services must be available over a RESTful API

classification.justification: Defines a required external interface (RESTful API) for core services, implying availability and interoperability.

REQ-0022.original_text: The QDR must be able to curate data collected in batches from the Industrial-Equipment Network
REQ-0022.final_text: The QDR must be able to curate data collected in batches from the Industrial-Equipment Network

classification.justification: Describes a core capability of the QDR system to process incoming data batches.

REQ-0023.original_text: At a predetermined time each calendar day, the QDR must process all curated data and populate a list of available elements for query
REQ-0023.final_text: At a predetermined time each calendar day, the QDR must process all curated data and populate a list of available elements for query

classification.justification: Describes a scheduled, recurring capability (processing and populating a list) that defines a system action.

REQ-0024.original_text: A range of short document types (e.g., guidelines, tutorials, frequently asked questions) in HyperText Markup Language (HTML) and/or Portable Document Format (PDF) format must describe the use of the software system.
REQ-0024.final_text: A range of short document types (e.g., guidelines, tutorials, frequently asked questions) in HyperText Markup Language (HTML) and/or Portable Document Format (PDF) format must describe the use of the software system.

classification.justification: Defines a capability (documentation) that must adhere to specific external data formats.

REQ-0025.original_text: The UI for the VDS must comply with the requirements of the application that executes the MTConnect Agent as defined by the standard documentation [1].
REQ-0025.final_text: The UI for the VDS must comply with the requirements of the application that executes the MTConnect Agent as defined by the standard documentation [1].

classification.justification: The requirement mandates adherence to an external standard's interface specifications.

REQ-0026.original_text: All pages of the QDR UI should employ an organizational method that separates the major sections of the application for easy access.
REQ-0026.final_text: All pages of the QDR UI should employ an organizational method that separates the major sections of the application for easy access.

classification.justification: Describes a capability related to the user experience, specifically ease of access.

REQ-0027.original_text: The user should be able to determine the sections of the QDR UI currently being viewed using visual cues, such as bold fonts, arrows, and highlighting.
REQ-0027.final_text: The user should be able to determine the sections of the QDR UI currently being viewed using visual cues, such as bold fonts, arrows, and highlighting.

classification.justification: Describes a user-facing capability related to the interface's presentation and ease of use.

REQ-0028.original_text: The application's major sections should be available from any page of the QDR and include the following pages: Query, Help, Introduction, Administration, Login, and Logout.
REQ-0028.final_text: The application's major sections should be available from any page of the QDR and include the following pages: Query, Help, Introduction, Administration, Login, and Logout.

classification.justification: This requirement specifies the required composition and navigation structure of the application's user interface.

REQ-0029.original_text: The user must be presented with a login page when accessing any page, expect a landing or front page, as an anonymous user.
REQ-0029.final_text: The user must be presented with a login page when accessing any page, expect a landing or front page, as an anonymous user.

classification.justification: Defines a required user experience flow (presenting a login page) under specific conditions.

REQ-0030.original_text: Administrative tasks and running queries must have a UI.
REQ-0030.final_text: Administrative tasks and running queries must have a UI.

classification.justification: States a requirement for a user interface to support specific system capabilities.

REQ-0031.original_text: The UI for administrative tasks must included the ability to manage Users, manage Groups, manage permissions, manage data templates, manage group assignments, and manage query templates.
REQ-0031.final_text: The UI for administrative tasks must included the ability to manage Users, manage Groups, manage permissions, manage data templates, manage group assignments, and manage query templates.

classification.justification: This requirement lists several distinct administrative capabilities the system must provide.

REQ-0032.original_text: The UI for running queries may include the ability to query the database using query templates defined by the administrator.
REQ-0032.final_text: The UI for running queries may include the ability to query the database using query templates defined by the administrator.

classification.justification: Describes a capability (querying) that utilizes a defined external structure (templates).

REQ-0033.original_text: All pages should use a consistent visual theme.
REQ-0033.final_text: All pages should use a consistent visual theme.

classification.justification: This requirement imposes a quality bound on the user experience regarding visual consistency.

REQ-0034.original_text: Pop-ups displaying notifications, error messages, or warnings should use the same theme and template as the rest of the application.
REQ-0034.final_text: Pop-ups displaying notifications, error messages, or warnings should use the same theme and template as the rest of the application.

classification.justification: This requirement imposes a design constraint on the visual presentation to ensure consistency.

REQ-0035.original_text: All server-side components must execute on server-class computers.
REQ-0035.final_text: All server-side components must execute on server-class computers.

classification.justification: Assigns all server-side components to a specific class of execution resource.

REQ-0036.original_text: All client-side components must execute on workstation-class and personal-class computers.
REQ-0036.final_text: All client-side components must execute on workstation-class and personal-class computers.

classification.justification: This requirement imposes a bound on the execution environment (resource) for the client components.

REQ-0037.original_text: The software interface should follow the Model-View-Controller (MVC) model for rendering and modeling data objects.
REQ-0037.final_text: The software interface should follow the Model-View-Controller (MVC) model for rendering and modeling data objects.

classification.justification: Specifies a required architectural pattern (MVC) for data modeling and rendering, which relates to how components interact.

REQ-0038.original_text: The interface must be able to connect to a database to store XML schema defined using XSD and data streams.
REQ-0038.final_text: The interface must be able to connect to a database to store XML schema defined using XSD and data streams.

classification.justification: Defines an external connection capability that must handle specific data formats (XML/XSD) and streams.

REQ-0039.original_text: Source and destination formats for data must include XML and may also include: Extensible Stylesheet Lanaguage Transformation (XSLT), JavaScript Object Notation (JSON), Comma Separated Value (CSV), and American Standard Code for Information Interchange (ASCII).
REQ-0039.final_text: Source and destination formats for data must include XML and may also include: Extensible Stylesheet Lanaguage Transformation (XSLT), JavaScript Object Notation (JSON), Comma Separated Value (CSV), and American Standard Code for Information Interchange (ASCII).

classification.justification: Specifies the required data formats for external exchanges, ensuring interoperability.

REQ-0045.original_text: Client-side UI applications must run within a web browser and not require any additional software interfaces on the client.
REQ-0045.final_text: Client-side UI applications must run within a web browser and not require any additional software interfaces on the client.

classification.justification: This requirement constrains the deployment environment, ensuring it runs within a standard browser without external dependencies.

REQ-0046.original_text: The communication architecture must follow the client-server model.
REQ-0046.final_text: The communication architecture must follow the client-server model.

classification.justification: This requirement specifies the high-level architectural pattern or composition of the system.

REQ-0047.original_text: Communication between the client and server should utilize a REST-compliant web service and must be served over HTTP Secure (HTTPS).
REQ-0047.final_text: Communication between the client and server should utilize a REST-compliant web service and must be served over HTTP Secure (HTTPS).

classification.justification: Defines the required communication protocol (REST/HTTPS) for external interaction.

REQ-0048.original_text: The client-server communication must be stateless.
REQ-0048.final_text: The client-server communication must be stateless.

classification.justification: This requirement imposes a specific architectural constraint on the nature of the communication protocol between systems.

REQ-0049.original_text: A uniform interface must separate the client roles from the server roles.
REQ-0049.final_text: A uniform interface must separate the client roles from the server roles.

classification.justification: This requirement defines a necessary separation boundary via a uniform interface between two defined roles.

REQ-0050.original_text: Communication over the VDS must conform to a reference MTConnect data schema [2] using XML.
REQ-0050.final_text: Communication over the VDS must conform to a reference MTConnect data schema [2] using XML.

classification.justification: Defines the required data format (XML/MTConnect schema) for external communication.

REQ-0051.original_text: Communication over the QDR must provide the user with the option of formatted ASCII CSV, JSON, or XML structured with a reference MTConnect data schema [2].
REQ-0051.final_text: Communication over the QDR must provide the user with the option of formatted ASCII CSV, JSON, or XML structured with a reference MTConnect data schema [2].

classification.justification: Defines the supported data formats and schema for an external communication interface.

REQ-0052.original_text: The VDS component of the software system must comply with all enumerated requirements for an application to execute an MTConnect Agent in the standard documentation [1].
REQ-0052.final_text: The VDS component of the software system must comply with all enumerated requirements for an application to execute an MTConnect Agent in the standard documentation [1].

classification.justification: This requirement mandates that a specific component must adhere to external standards, which acts as a compliance and interoperability constraint.

REQ-0053.original_text: The QDR component of the software system must retrieve data from an indexed database based on a user-defined query request via a template-driven web-based user interface, an endpoint query, and/or a RESTful API call.
REQ-0053.final_text: The QDR component of the software system must retrieve data from an indexed database based on a user-defined query request via a template-driven web-based user interface, an endpoint query, and/or a RESTful API call.

classification.justification: This requirement describes a capability (data retrieval) that must interact with multiple external interfaces (UI, endpoint, API).

REQ-0054.original_text: The QDR must provide the administrator with the ability to predefine queries for use by non-administrator users.
REQ-0054.final_text: The QDR must provide the administrator with the ability to predefine queries for use by non-administrator users.

classification.justification: Describes a capability (predefine queries) that is exposed to a specific user role (administrator/non-administrator).

REQ-0055.original_text: The QDR must enable the administrator to limit query results by date ranges and/or number of records.
REQ-0055.final_text: The QDR must enable the administrator to limit query results by date ranges and/or number of records.

classification.justification: Describes a capability provided to an administrator, which implies an interface for inputting limits.

REQ-0056.original_text: When configured in a query by the administrator, the QDR must provide the user a list of all available devices in the database and all available associated data elements for that device.
REQ-0056.final_text: When configured in a query by the administrator, the QDR must provide the user a list of all available devices in the database and all available associated data elements for that device.

classification.justification: This describes a capability (providing a list) that interacts with the database/system via a query interface.

REQ-0057.original_text: The user may be provided the ability to select what data elements to retrieve via the predefined query.
REQ-0057.final_text: The user may be provided the ability to select what data elements to retrieve via the predefined query.

classification.justification: Describes a capability (selecting data elements) that is exposed via a predefined query interface.

REQ-0058.original_text: The user must have the ability to select the time range and/or number of records for the data to retrieve via the query.
REQ-0058.final_text: The user must have the ability to select the time range and/or number of records for the data to retrieve via the query.

classification.justification: Describes a user capability that dictates parameters for an external data retrieval operation.

REQ-0059.original_text: The user must have the ability to retrieve and view data as formatted ASCII CSV, JSON, or XML.
REQ-0059.final_text: The user must have the ability to retrieve and view data as formatted ASCII CSV, JSON, or XML.

classification.justification: Defines a capability to retrieve data while specifying multiple acceptable external data formats.

REQ-0060.original_text: The user must have the ability to download retrieved data as ASCII CSV, JSON, or XML.
REQ-0060.final_text: The user must have the ability to download retrieved data as ASCII CSV, JSON, or XML.

classification.justification: Defines a capability (download) that specifies multiple allowed output formats.

REQ-0061.original_text: The software system must provide a function to store and manage data in a database according to a data-structure template (e.g., schema, ontology).
REQ-0061.final_text: The software system must provide a function to store and manage data in a database according to a data-structure template (e.g., schema, ontology).

classification.justification: The requirement describes a capability (store/manage data) whose primary focus is adherence to a specific data structure template.

REQ-0062.original_text: The software system must provide a RESTful API for curating data in the database.
REQ-0062.final_text: The software system must provide a RESTful API for curating data in the database.

classification.justification: Defines an external API surface (RESTful) for a core system capability (curating data).

REQ-0063.original_text: The software system must provide the data curator with the ability to add data to the database in accordance with a stored data-structure template.
REQ-0063.final_text: The software system must provide the data curator with the ability to add data to the database in accordance with a stored data-structure template.

classification.justification: Describes a capability (adding data) that must adhere to a specific data structure template.

REQ-0064.original_text: The software system may provide all curation functionality via a web-based user interface.
REQ-0064.final_text: The software system may provide all curation functionality via a web-based user interface.

classification.justification: Describes a capability (curation) delivered through a specific user-facing interface.

REQ-0065.original_text: The software system must provide a function to manage user accounts and data-structure templates (e.g., schemas, ontologies).
REQ-0065.final_text: The software system must provide a function to manage user accounts and data-structure templates (e.g., schemas, ontologies).

classification.justification: The requirement describes a capability (manage accounts/templates) that implies the existence of underlying structural components to support it.

REQ-0066.original_text: The software system must provide a web-based UI to CRUD manage users.
REQ-0066.final_text: The software system must provide a web-based UI to CRUD manage users.

classification.justification: Defines a user-facing capability (CRUD) that requires a specific interface type (web-based UI).

REQ-0067.original_text: The software system must provide the administrator with the ability to CRUD manage users.
REQ-0067.final_text: The software system must provide the administrator with the ability to CRUD manage users.

classification.justification: This requirement describes a core capability the system must provide to an administrator.

REQ-0068.original_text: The software system must provide the administrator with the ability to CRUD manage groups.
REQ-0068.final_text: The software system must provide the administrator with the ability to CRUD manage groups.

classification.justification: This requirement describes a core capability the system must provide to an administrator.

REQ-0069.original_text: The software system must provide the administrator with the ability to CRUD manage user group assignments.
REQ-0069.final_text: The software system must provide the administrator with the ability to CRUD manage user group assignments.

classification.justification: This requirement describes a core capability the system must provide to an administrator.

REQ-0070.original_text: The software system must provide the administrator with the ability to CRUD manage permissions assigned to users and groups.
REQ-0070.final_text: The software system must provide the administrator with the ability to CRUD manage permissions assigned to users and groups.

classification.justification: This requirement describes a core capability the system must provide to an administrator.

REQ-0071.original_text: The software system must provide a web-based user interface for CRUD manage data templates.
REQ-0071.final_text: The software system must provide a web-based user interface for CRUD manage data templates.

classification.justification: Defines a user-facing capability (CRUD) that requires a specific presentation mechanism (web-based UI).

REQ-0072.original_text: The software system must provide the administrator with the ability to CRUD manage data-structure templates using the XSD format.
REQ-0072.final_text: The software system must provide the administrator with the ability to CRUD manage data-structure templates using the XSD format.

classification.justification: Defines a capability (CRUD management) that requires adherence to a specific external data format (XSD).

REQ-0073.original_text: The software system may provide all administration functionality via a RESTful API.
REQ-0073.final_text: The software system may provide all administration functionality via a RESTful API.

classification.justification: Defines the system's external interaction surface using a specific protocol (RESTful API).

REQ-0074.original_text: The software system must allow the administrator to create a Universally Unique Identifier (UUID) associated with a device.
REQ-0074.final_text: The software system must allow the administrator to create a Universally Unique Identifier (UUID) associated with a device.

classification.justification: This requirement describes a capability the system must perform for an administrator.

REQ-0075.original_text: The software system must require a UUID to be provided with any data streams uploaded.
REQ-0075.final_text: The software system must require a UUID to be provided with any data streams uploaded.

classification.justification: This requirement mandates a specific data format (UUID) for all incoming data streams, acting as an interface constraint.

REQ-0076.original_text: The VDS must provide available fresh data to the requesting client at a rate of no less than 1 Hz.
REQ-0076.final_text: The VDS must provide available fresh data to the requesting client at a rate of no less than 1 Hz.

classification.justification: This requirement defines a capability (providing data) that is strictly bounded by a minimum rate (1 Hz) and availability.

REQ-0081.original_text: The software system defined in this SRS must follow industry recommended practices for secure software development.
REQ-0081.final_text: The software system defined in this SRS must follow industry recommended practices for secure software development.

classification.justification: This requirement imposes a quality bound on the entire development process based on industry standards.

REQ-0082.original_text: At a minimum, the software development must practice the principle of least privilege for defining access-level requirements of the software system and its associated services.
REQ-0082.final_text: At a minimum, the software development must practice the principle of least privilege for defining access-level requirements of the software system and its associated services.

classification.justification: This requirement mandates a security principle (least privilege) that acts as a binding rule for access control.

REQ-0083.original_text: The production-release version of the software system must pass an automated dynamic application security testing tool (e.g., HP WEBINSPECT).
REQ-0083.final_text: The production-release version of the software system must pass an automated dynamic application security testing tool (e.g., HP WEBINSPECT).

classification.justification: This requirement imposes a mandatory quality gate (a bound) related to security testing compliance.

REQ-0084.original_text: The architecture, design, implementation, and documentation of the application must minimize the maintenance costs of the software system.
REQ-0084.final_text: The architecture, design, implementation, and documentation of the application must minimize the maintenance costs of the software system.

classification.justification: This requirement sets a quality budget on the overall development process, specifically targeting maintenance cost.

REQ-0085.original_text: The maximum person-time required to fi x a security defect (including regression testing and documentation update) must not exceed two person days.
REQ-0085.final_text: The maximum person-time required to fi x a security defect (including regression testing and documentation update) must not exceed two person days.

classification.justification: This requirement sets a time bound on the effort required for maintenance activities.

REQ-0086.original_text: Otherwise, the software system must be taken offline or the offending feature disabled.
REQ-0086.final_text: Otherwise, the software system must be taken offline or the offending feature disabled.

classification.justification: This requirement mandates a specific recovery action (taking offline or disabling) when a failure occurs, defining a behavioral constraint on system availability.

REQ-0087.original_text: The average person-time required to make a minor enhancement (including testing and documentation update) must not exceed one person week.
REQ-0087.final_text: The average person-time required to make a minor enhancement (including testing and documentation update) must not exceed one person week.

classification.justification: This requirement sets a bound on the effort (time/cost) required for maintenance activities.

REQ-0088.original_text: The system must have at least a Super-User role and a User role defined for accessing and interacting with the system.
REQ-0088.final_text: The system must have at least a Super-User role and a User role defined for accessing and interacting with the system.

classification.justification: Defines the required set of roles/access levels that constitute the system's operational structure.

REQ-0089.original_text: Additional roles may be defined for the system as long as the business rules for the administrator and user roles are satisfied.
REQ-0089.final_text: Additional roles may be defined for the system as long as the business rules for the administrator and user roles are satisfied.

classification.justification: This requirement imposes a constraint on the definition of new roles based on existing business rules.

REQ-0090.original_text: At a minimum, the Super-User role must account for the data explorer , data curator , and administrator accounttype requirements described in Section 4.3.
REQ-0090.final_text: At a minimum, the Super-User role must account for the data explorer , data curator , and administrator accounttype requirements described in Section 4.3.

classification.justification: This requirement allocates the Super-User role to encompass several defined account types.

REQ-0091.original_text: At a minimum, the User role must account for the data explorer account-type requirements described in Section 4.3.
REQ-0091.final_text: At a minimum, the User role must account for the data explorer account-type requirements described in Section 4.3.

classification.justification: This requirement allocates the User role to adhere to the data explorer account-type requirements defined elsewhere.

REQ-0092.original_text: SuperUserRole/001 Maintains all VDS and QDR back-end system configurations
REQ-0092.final_text: SuperUserRole/001 Maintains all VDS and QDR back-end system configurations

classification.justification: Describes a maintenance capability performed by a specific role.

REQ-0093.original_text: SuperUserRole/002 Maintains all VDS and QDR schemas and templates
REQ-0093.final_text: SuperUserRole/002 Maintains all VDS and QDR schemas and templates

classification.justification: Defines a maintenance capability related to the structure (schemas/templates) of the system data.

REQ-0094.original_text: SuperUserRole/003 Maintains all user groups and user accounts
REQ-0094.final_text: SuperUserRole/003 Maintains all user groups and user accounts

classification.justification: This requirement describes a core administrative capability that the system must perform.

REQ-0095.original_text: SuperUserRole/004 Maintains all QDR predefined queries
REQ-0095.final_text: SuperUserRole/004 Maintains all QDR predefined queries

classification.justification: This requirement describes a capability or action that a specific role maintains.

REQ-0096.original_text: UserRole/001 Connects to and retrieves and reads data from VDS and QDR in conformance with the maintained VDS and QDR schemas and templates
REQ-0096.final_text: UserRole/001 Connects to and retrieves and reads data from VDS and QDR in conformance with the maintained VDS and QDR schemas and templates

classification.justification: Defines an interaction (connect/retrieve) with external data sources while adhering to specific data formats.
